name: Bhai Amarjit Singh Taan
body: Belonging to the Rababi heritage of music, Bhai Amarjit Singh Taan received his musical education from his grandfather, Bhai Mehar Singh Taan. The title ‘Taan’ is said to have been bestowed upon Bhai Mehar Singh during a program in Kashmir organised by Sant Sunder Singh, who was very pleased with Bhai Mehar Singh’s singing, especially his delivery of taans. However, it should also be noted that Bhai Mehar Singh’s father was Bhai Taana Singh, who was very popular in his time. Some sources say that the title ‘Taan’ originated from Bhai Taana Singh’s name. Either way, it is apparent that talent flows in this family and Bhai Amarjit Singh Taan is no exception. He has inherited the virtuosity of his elders and is blessed with a very sweet but commanding voice. Bhai Amarjit Singh Taan has been performing Kirtan for a very long time and has traveled to many countries around the world.

Most of the recordings below are from his tour to South East Asia between 1981 and 1982. From my own personal experience, I have heard him perform live in 2011 during a very short visit to Malaysia. In true Rababi manner, he possesses a very powerful voice that can reach a great distance without the use of a microphone or speakers. In no way has age changed the quality of his voice. He sounded almost the same as he did 30 years ago.
If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Amarjit Singh Taan to share, please contact us.
